behaviors.description: The traveling-salesman heuristic produces a route that is no more than 1.5x the length of the optimal route for inputs up to 20 stops.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Unit, because this behavior is algorithm-quality at one function boundary (heuristic output vs computed optimal) with no stated I/O. Pass 2 downgrade check: lower than Unit is not possible. Upgrade check: Integration/System/Agentic/Workflow are not required by the behavior text; it does not mention module handoff failures, real OS/network/subprocess, or AI output. Rejection-gate: higher levels were rejected because the behavior only states route-length ratio correctness for bounded inputs.
behaviors.test_description: Call the heuristic function with deterministic coordinate sets (sizes 3..20), compute exact optimal length in-test (brute force/DP for bounded N), and assert heuristic_length/optimal_length <= 1.5 for each case.

behaviors.description: The delivery time window estimates are computed correctly from the distance between consecutive stops and the configured average speed for the road type.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Unit, since this is formula/config application logic (distance and speed by road type). Pass 2 downgrade check: cannot go below Unit. Upgrade check: no explicit behavior text requiring real infra or cross-module failure boundary; mocked dependencies would not hide the core arithmetic/config bug here. Rejection-gate: Integration/System/Agentic/Workflow disqualified because behavior text is purely computational.
behaviors.test_description: Test the estimator function with fixed legs (distance, road_type, speed config) and assert per-leg travel time and cumulative windows match expected values, including mixed road types and rounding boundaries.

behaviors.description: The shift-hours validator rejects a route whose total estimated time exceeds the driver's remaining shift hours, returning the excess amount.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Unit, as this is a validator rule on two inputs (total_estimated_time, remaining_shift_hours) plus excess calculation. Pass 2 downgrade check: not possible below Unit. Upgrade check: no stated module interaction, real infra, or AI dependence in this behavior. Rejection-gate: higher levels rejected because behavior text is a direct decision/calculation rule.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke validator with boundary cases (equal, just under, just over, far over shift hours) and assert reject/accept flag plus exact excess amount.

behaviors.description: When one address in the list fails geocoding (API returns no results), the service skips that stop and optimizes the remaining addresses, reporting which addresses were skipped.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration, because the failure appears at a module boundary: geocoding result handling must propagate into optimizer input and skipped-address reporting. Pass 2 downgrade check: Unit is insufficient for this stated behavior because it requires interaction of geocoding adapter result, route optimization service flow, and reporting output. Upgrade check: System not required by text; it says 'API returns no results' but does not require real HTTP/network behavior, and an in-process geocoder stub returning empty results reproduces the failure mode. Agentic/Workflow disqualified: no AI output and no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service + real orchestration modules with an in-process stub geocoder that returns coordinates for valid addresses and empty for one address; assert skipped list contains that address, optimizer receives only geocoded stops, and final route excludes skipped stop.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration is required because assertions span module handoff (geocoder -> optimizer -> report), not a single function.

behaviors.description: Duplicate addresses in the input list are consolidated into a single stop with combined delivery items, not visited twice.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration, since behavior spans deduplication/aggregation plus route-planning consumption ('not visited twice'). Pass 2 downgrade check: Unit could verify only consolidation logic, but the full stated behavior includes downstream visit count, which is a module interaction boundary. Upgrade check: no explicit need for real OS/network/subprocess; no AI dependence; no multi-agentic sequence. Rejection-gate: System/Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior text only requires local module wiring and data flow correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Run service pipeline with input containing duplicate addresses and distinct item payloads; assert output stop list has one consolidated stop with merged items and the produced route includes that stop exactly once.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration level matches the test, which validates both consolidation and downstream planner behavior together.
